speaker: DiFrancisco, Michael
text: The group has agreed to apply the ITU two-point criteria for the FSS protection analysis. We will use negative 10.5 dB at the 80th percentile and negative 6 dB at 0.03 percent. Are there any objections? Hearing none, that is approved.

speaker: LaSorte, Nickolas
text: I want to note for the record that the single-point criterion of negative 10.5 dB at 0.03 percent would provide equivalent protection to the ITU two-point standard. This is a technical claim based on the mathematical relationship between the two thresholds.

speaker: Nolen, Katrece - Contractor
text: Nick, can you send the FSS protection zone working paper to the group by Friday? We need it before the next meeting.

speaker: Bhatt, Sandip
text: The DoW course of action number three proposes reallocation of the 7125 to 7250 MHz band segment. This would affect approximately 847 FSS earth stations currently operating under primary status.

speaker: DiFrancisco, Michael
text: We will defer the decision on COA selection to the next working group meeting. There is not enough information available today to make that determination.

speaker: McKenna, Paul
text: NTIA will coordinate with DoD to obtain the updated system parameters for the FSS earth stations in the 7 GHz band. This is an action item for NTIA before the next session.

speaker: LaSorte, Nickolas
text: The working group has noted the comments submitted by the DoW on the study plan. No changes to the study plan are approved at this time.

speaker: Trotman, Carlotta
text: Good morning everyone. Just checking in on the audio. Can everyone hear me clearly?

speaker: DiFrancisco, Michael
text: The group considered the proposal to include secondary FSS sites in the interference analysis. This remains an open question that will be addressed in the next working paper.

speaker: Nolen, Katrece - Contractor
text: Action item for the DoW team: provide updated propagation model parameters to the TIG by end of month.
